title: InstCombine: Infer nnan/ninf on canonicalize
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by shifting from a pattern-specific check to a data-driven inference of fast-math flags (`FMF`). Originally, the transform removed `llvm.canonicalize` only when the result couldn't be `inf`. The patch generalizes this by using `KnownFPClasses` from both the call result and its operand to infer strengthened `FMF` (like `nnan` or `ninf`). If inferred flags differ from existing ones, it updates the call accordingly and drops UB-implying attributes.

This approach abstracts the core insight: `nofpclass` constraints on a `canonicalize` result can imply specific fast-math flags on the call itself. By leveraging existing `KnownFPClasses` analysis, the transform now handles any `nofpclass` restriction (e.g., `nan`, `sub`, `zero`) uniformly, eliminating the need for special-case logic. Developers can apply similar strategies by using demanded/known value analysis to infer and propagate attributes generically.
